vulnerability.reason: Reference describes incentives from automated scoring to overfit to public tests; generated describes inability of programmers to verify generalization. Different sensitivity conditions.
vulnerability.reference: Automated scoring on visible test cases creates incentives to optimize for passing those tests without ensuring general correctness.
vulnerability.generated: The contest evaluation gap between public and hidden test cases leaves programmers unable to verify generalization of AI-generated code.

impact.reason: Reference mentions loss of trust and proliferation of brittle code that misleads users; generated mentions lower rankings and wasted effort. Damage types differ, and affected party is only programmers.
impact.reference: Loss of trust in competition fairness and proliferation of brittle, overfit code that fails hidden tests, misleading users.
impact.generated: Programmers suffer lower contest rankings and wasted effort when overfitted solutions fail hidden test evaluation.

dose_response.reason: Generated captures overfitting leading to hidden-test failure and wasted effort, but omits the causal links to unfair outcomes, loss of trust, and brittle code proliferation, truncating the arc.
dose_response.reference: Overfitting to public test cases enables AI-generated code that passes visible tests but fails hidden ones, which in ranked contests exposes participants to unfair outcomes, leading to loss of trust and proliferation of brittle code.
dose_response.generated: RL-based code synthesis that overfits to finite execution feedback, when employed by programmers in ranked contests, exploits the gap between public and hidden test cases, leading to lower rankings and wasted effort from hidden test failures.
